label: people | perish | save
sentence: Once upon a time, in a quiet Germanic realm cloaked by endless forests and misty moors, all the people had perished save for two helpless children—a baby boy and his elder sister.

label: parent | die | ∅
sentence: Their parents had died while they slept, leaving the siblings to awaken alone in a small timbered hut by the edge of the Schwarzwald.

label: girl | be | first
sentence: The girl, a calm and watchful child, was the first to rouse.

label: she | look | ∅
sentence: She looked around and, seeing no one but her little brother smiling in his dreams, lay back down in the straw-filled bed.

label: day | pass | ∅
sentence: Ten days passed, and the boy shifted slightly without opening his eyes.

label: radiance | hover | watch
sentence: Yet in those quiet watches, a faint, silvery radiance hovered about his head, lighting the dark hut with an otherworldly glow that puzzled his sister.

label: growth | be | ∅
sentence: As the seasons turned, the girl grew into a young woman; meanwhile, the boy’s growth was slow and measured.

label: year | go | youth
sentence: Many years went by before he could crawl, and well into his youth before he could stand unaided.

label: sister | craft | bow
sentence: When he finally learned to walk, his sister crafted a small bow and arrow for him, and hung a tiny snail shell around his neck, softly saying, “Your name shall be Schneckenkind—He of the Little Shell.”

label: boy | venture | wood
sentence: Every day, the boy ventured into the dense woods with his bow, aiming at the small creatures of the forest.

label: bird | be | tit
sentence: The first bird he felled was a lively coal tit.

label: sister | be | ∅
sentence: His sister was delighted and carefully preserved the delicate creature as a trophy.

label: he | catch | squirrel
sentence: On the next day, he caught a red squirrel, which she also saved.

label: partridge | provide | meal
sentence: By the third day, a wood partridge provided the evening meal.

label: schneckenkind | roam | courage
sentence: With newfound courage, Schneckenkind roamed further into the forest.

label: skill | grow | ∅
sentence: His skill grew as he hunted deer, boars, and even bears.

label: he | become | woodsman
sentence: Though small, he became a master woodsman, bringing back all he caught to share with his sister.

label: light | dance | head
sentence: Each time he entered their lodge, that same mysterious light danced about his head, filling the room with a strange, silver splendor.

label: he | remain | ∅
sentence: Though Schneckenkind had reached the age of a man in years, he remained as small as a child.

label: he | spy | figure
sentence: One winter’s day, wandering near a frozen lake, he spied a giant figure busy trapping beavers on the ice.

label: giant | be | ∅
sentence: The giant was broad-shouldered, clad in a heavy fur coat lined with wolf pelts, and wielded a massive spear.

label: schneckenkind | creep | ∅
sentence: After the giant had hunted many beavers and hauled them onto a wooden sled, Schneckenkind crept alongside and, wielding his enchanted shell, deftly snipped off the tail of one beaver before slipping away.

label: he | baffle | ∅
sentence: When the giant returned, he was baffled to find one of his catches missing its tail, muttering curses in his guttural voice.

label: schneckenkind | repeat | feat
sentence: The next day, Schneckenkind repeated the feat, again stealing a beaver’s tail from under the giant’s nose.

label: giant | swear | ∅
sentence: Furious, the giant swore he would find and punish the elusive thief.

label: magic | make | ∅
sentence: Yet, despite his keen eyes and heavy footsteps, he found no trace—Schneckenkind’s magic made him invisible and silent as the forest shadows.

label: giant | arrive | day
sentence: On the following day, the giant arrived early, hoping to trap the little man.

label: he | chase | they
sentence: Hunting the fresh tracks, he chased them through snow and ice, but just as he neared his hut, he caught sight of Schneckenkind—who allowed himself to be seen.

label: giant | growl | ∅
sentence: The giant growled thunderously, “Who are you, little man?

label: i | mean | ∅
sentence: I mean to vanquish you.”

label: schneckenkind | reply | bravery
sentence: But Schneckenkind replied with calm bravery, “You will never catch me.”

label: giant | lunge | ∅
sentence: The giant lunged, but Schnneckenkind vanished like mist.

label: ∅ | demand | giant
sentence: “Where are you now?” demanded the giant.

label: voice | come | belt
sentence: “Under your belt,” came the voice.

label: giant | strike | hand
sentence: The giant struck his broad hand down, but found only empty air.

label: giant | roar | ∅
sentence: the giant roared.

label: voice | tease | ∅
sentence: the voice teased.

label: giant | grab | nose
sentence: The giant grabbed at his nose, only to hear the tiny voice laughing far away on the snowy ground.

label: ∅ | count | tail
sentence: Count your beaver tails—and know that I have taken one for my sister, too.”

label: light | return | ∅
sentence: As he vanished, the silver light returned, bathing the snow in a magical glow, a sight the slow-witted giant could not comprehend.

label: schneckenkind | tell | sister
sentence: Returning home, Schneckenkind told his sister that their time together was nearing its end.

label: it | be | fate
sentence: “I must journey away; it is my fate.

label: she | say | ∅
sentence:
Tell me, where would you wish to dwell?”

“My heart longs for the place where dawn breaks—the eastern sky,” she said softly.

label: ∅ | think | i
sentence: When you see the painted skies, think of me, shining bright.”

label: ∅ | make | home
sentence: “And I,” said Schneckenkind, “will make my home on the highest mountains, where streams run clear and the air is pure.

label: light | shine | head
sentence: There, golden light will shine forever around my head, and they will call me Berggeist—the Little Wild Man of the Mountains.

label: i | seek | spirit
sentence: But before we part, I must seek out the spirits who rule the earth and learn who will be friend or foe.”

label: he | travel | land
sentence: So he traveled far across the land and even descended into the deep earth, where a fierce giant spirit kept a boiling cauldron.

label: giant | recognize | schneckenkind
sentence: This giant, kin to the one who hunted beavers, recognized Schneckenkind and cast him into the kettle to drown.

label: schneckenkind | empty | cauldron
sentence: But with the power of his magic shell, Schneckenkind emptied the cauldron in an instant and leapt free, unharmed.

label: he | return | sister
sentence: He returned to his sister and told her all he had seen.

label: ∅ | dwell | spirit
sentence: “At each of the four corners of the earth dwells a spirit, and above them all a Great Being who decides our fate.

label: ∅ | be | spirit
sentence: There is also a wicked spirit deep beneath the earth to avoid.

label: you | carry | ∅
sentence: When the winds blow from the four corners, you must go—you will be carried to where you long to be.

label: i | dwell | height
sentence:
I shall dwell among the rocky heights, where my kin delight.”

With that, Schneckenkind took his bow and set out up a towering mountain.

label: ∅ | blow | ∅
sentence: Blow, winds, blow!

label: sister | linger | ∅
sentence:
My sister lingers 
Where the dawn with rosy fingers 
Paints the skies on morning’s breath, 
Bathing clouds in shades of death.

label: ∅ | guide | wood
sentence:
There her gleaming face I’ll see, 
Guiding through the wood and lea.

label: ∅ | sing | wild
sentence:
While I roam the mountain peaks, 
Sing my wild and wordless speaks.

label: wind | rise | ∅
sentence: Soon the winds rose, swirling through the forest, lifting his sister toward the blush of dawn.

label: she | remain | light
sentence: There she remains still, a shining light in the morning sky—the Morning Star of the Schwarzwald, forever watching.
